methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $5.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
TAE Technologies is a Series G climate and energy company founded by Norman Rostoker and Michl Binderbauer, currently carrying a $6.0B post-money valuation on $1.3B+ raised. The relevant peer universe comprises 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private peers at the same or adjacent stage, including Helion ($5.4B Series F), Form Energy ($3.5B Series G), Sila Nanotechnologies ($3.3B), and Watershed ($1.8B Series G).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $6.0B last post-money and applying a 1.25x multiplier typical of Series G advancement, yields $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across the four comparable companies produced a median of $3.4B, with a range spanning $1.8B to $5.4B. This dispersion reflects variance in technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market positioning within the sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $2.9B to $8.6B, with a median of $5.5B. The current $6.0B post-money sits at the upper-middle portion of this distribution. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiplier for Series G to later stages and whether the peer-median is materially depressed or inflated by outliers (Helion at the high end, Watershed at the low end). The wide range reflects structural uncertainty around path-to-commercialization timelines and capital requirements across the climate-tech sector.
